2603.17380.title: SCALE:Scalable Conditional Atlas-Level Endpoint transport for virtual cell perturbation prediction
2603.17380.paper_summary: Virtual cell models aim to enable in silico experimentation by predicting how cells respond to genetic, chemical, or cytokine perturbations from single-cell measurements. In practice, however, large-scale perturbation prediction remains constrained by three coupled bottlenecks: inefficient training and inference pipelines, unstable modeling in high-dimensional sparse expression space, and evaluation protocols that overemphasize reconstruction-like accuracy while underestimating biological fidelity. In this work we present a specialized large-scale foundation model SCALE for virtual cell perturbation prediction that addresses the above limitations jointly. First, we build a BioNeMo-based training and inference framework that substantially improves data throughput, distributed scalability, and deployment efficiency, yielding 12.51* speedup on pretrain and 1.29* on inference over the prior SOTA pipeline under matched system settings. Second, we formulate perturbation prediction as conditional transport and implement it with a set-aware flow architecture that couples LLaMA-based cellular encoding with endpoint-oriented supervision. This design yields more stable training and stronger recovery of perturbation effects. Third, we evaluate the model on Tahoe-100M using a rigorous cell-level protocol centered on biologically meaningful metrics rather than reconstruction alone. On this benchmark, our model improves PDCorr by 12.02% and DE Overlap by 10.66% over STATE. Together, these results suggest that advancing virtual cells requires not only better generative objectives, but also the co-design of scalable infrastructure, stable transport modeling, and biologically faithful evaluation.
2603.17380.paper_summary_zh: 虛擬細胞模型旨在通過預測細胞如何對基因、化學或細胞因子的擾動做出反應，從單細胞測量中實現計算實驗。然而，在實踐中，大規模擾動預測仍然受到三個相互關聯的瓶頸的限制：低效的訓練和推斷管道、高維稀疏表達空間中的不穩定建模，以及過度強調重建類準確性而低估生物學真實性的評估協議。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個專門的大規模基礎模型SCALE，用於虛擬細胞擾動預測，旨在共同解決上述限制。首先，我們建立了一個基於BioNeMo的訓練和推斷框架，顯著提高了數據吞吐量、分佈式可擴展性和部署效率，在匹配的系統設置下，預訓練速度提升12.51倍，推斷速度提升1.29倍，優於先前的SOTA管道。其次，我們將擾動預測公式化為條件傳輸，並通過一個集合感知流架構來實現，該架構將基於LLaMA的細胞編碼與端點導向的監督結合起來。這一設計帶來了更穩定的訓練和更強的擾動效應恢復。第三，我們在Tahoe-100M上使用以生物學上有意義的指標為中心的嚴格細胞級協議對模型進行評估，而不僅僅是重建。在這一基準上，我們的模型在PDCorr上提高了12.02%，在DE重疊上提高了10.66%，優於STATE。綜合這些結果表明，推進虛擬細胞不僅需要更好的生成目標，還需要可擴展基礎設施、穩定的傳輸建模和生物學上真實的評估的共同設計。
2603.17380.author: Shuizhou Chen et.al.
2603.17380.authors: Shuizhou Chen, Lang Yu, Kedu Jin, Songming Zhang, Hao Wu, Wenxuan Huang, Sheng Xu, Quan Qian, Qin Chen, Lei Bai, Siqi Sun, Zhangyang Gao
